text: Robotic radical cystectomy with fluorine neobladder and renal graft nephrolitherectomy for ureteral carcinoma in a double renal transplant recipient.
The surgeon isolates and sections the right natal ureter, followed by the isolation and sectioning of the right renal graft pedicle. The right graft ureter is then isolated up to the bladder.

The surgeon isolates the left native ureter and the left graft filter, carefully preserving the integrity and maximizing the length of the graft filter despite the patient having a right solitary kidney.

The surgeon performs bilateral intrafascial nerve sparing. The membranous urethra is isolated and sectioned, resulting in the removal of the urothelium of the bladder, native urethra, and right renal graft.
The Florence robotic intracorporeal neobladder is constructed. The surgeon performs posterior reconstruction by suturing the Denonvilliers' fascia to the peritoneum using a 3-0 barbed suture.
The surgeon performs the urethroneal bladder anastomosis using two running hemisutures. Subsequently, the neobladder is de-tubolized and reconfigured following the flooring technique.
The surgeon performs a single urethral neobladder anastomosis, connecting the neobladder to the left graft urethra, ensuring precise alignment and secure suturing.
